name: minikube
description: Minikube provisions and manages local Kubernetes clusters optimized for development workflows

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Starts a local Kubernetes cluster

options.description: Enable addons. see `minikube addons list` for a list of valid addon names
A set of apiserver IP Addresses which are used in the generated certificate for kubernetes
The authoritative apiserver hostname for apiserver certificates and connectivity
A set of apiserver names which are used in the generated certificate for kubernetes
The apiserver listening port
If set, automatically updates drivers to the latest version
The base image to use for docker/podman drivers. Intended for local development
If true, cache docker images for the current bootstrapper and load them into the machine
Duration until minikube certificate expiration
CNI plug-in to use
The container runtime to be used
Number of CPUs allocated to Kubernetes
The cri socket path to be used
If set, delete the current cluster if start fails and try again
Disables the filesystem mounts provided by the hypervisors
Disk size allocated to the minikube VM
The cluster dns domain name used in the Kubernetes cluster
Enable proxy for NAT DNS requests
Environment variables to pass to the Docker daemon. (format: key=value)
Specify arbitrary flags to pass to the Docker daemon. (format: key=value)
If true, only download and cache files for later use - don't install or start anything
Choose driver
Dry-run mode. Validates configuration, but does not mutate system state
If true, will embed the certs in kubeconfig
A set of key=value pairs that describe configuration that may be passed to different components
Number of extra disks created and attached to the minikube VM
A set of key=value pairs that describe feature gates for alpha/experimental features
Force minikube to perform possibly dangerous operations
If set, force the container runtime to use systemd as cgroup manager
Enable host resolver for NAT DNS requests
The CIDR to be used for the minikube VM
NIC Type used for host only network
Location of the VPNKit socket used for networking
List of guest VSock ports that should be exposed as sockets on the host
External Adapter on which external switch will be created if no external switch is found
Whether to use external switch over Default Switch if virtual switch not explicitly specified
The hyperv virtual switch name. Defaults to first found
Country code of the image mirror to be used
Alternative image repository to pull docker images from
Insecure Docker registries to pass to the Docker daemon
If set, install addons. Defaults to true
Allow user prompts for more information
Locations to fetch the minikube ISO from
This will keep the existing kubectl context and will create a minikube context
The Kubernetes version that the minikube VM will use
Enable experimental NVIDIA GPU support in minikube
Hide the hypervisor signature from the guest in minikube
The KVM default network name
Simulate numa node count in minikube, supported numa node count range is 1-8
The KVM QEMU connection URI
IP Address to use to expose ports
Amount of RAM to allocate to Kubernetes
This will start the mount daemon and automatically mount files into minikube
The argument to pass the minikube mount command on start
The named space to activate after start
NIC Type used for nat network
Use native Golang SSH client
Network to run minikube with
Kubelet network plug-in to use
Local folders to share with Guest via NFS mounts
Where to root the NFS Shares
If set, minikube VM/container will start without starting or configuring Kubernetes
Disable checking for the availability of hardware virtualization before the vm is started
The number of nodes to spin up
Format to print stdout in
List of ports that should be exposed
If set, download tarball of preloaded images if available to improve start time
Registry mirrors to pass to the Docker daemon
The CIDR to be used for service cluster IPs
IP address
SSH key
SSH port
SSH user
Send trace events
Provide VM UUID to restore MAC address
Filter to use only VM Drivers
Comma separated list of Kubernetes components to verify and wait for after starting a cluster
Max time to wait per Kubernetes or host to be healthy

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Gets the status of a local Kubernetes cluster

options.description: Go template format string for the status output
Output layout
The node to check status for
Format to print stdout in
Continuously listing/getting the status with optional interval duration

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stops a running local Kubernetes cluster

options.description: Set flag to stop all profiles (clusters)
Output layout
The node to check status for
Format to print stdout in
Set flag to stop cluster after a set amount of time

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a local Kubernetes cluster

options.description: Set flag to delete all profiles
Set this flag to delete the '.minikube' folder from your user directory

subcommands.name: unpause
subcommands.description: Unpause Kubernetes

options.description: If set, unpause all namespaces
Namespaces to unpause
Format to print stdout in

subcommands.name: docker-env
subcommands.description: Configure environment to use minikube's Docker daemon

options.description: If set, unpause all namespaces
Format to print stdout in
Force environment to be configured for a specified shell
Use SSH connection instead of HTTPS (port 2376)
Unset variables instead of setting them

subcommands.name: podman-env
subcommands.description: Configure environment to use minikube's Podman service

options.description: Force environment to be configured for a specified shell
Unset variables instead of setting them

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: Add, delete, or push a local image into minikube

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add an image to local cache

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete an image from the local cache

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all available images from the local cache

subcommands.name: reload
subcommands.description: Reload cached images

subcommands.name: image
subcommands.description: Manage images

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build a container image in minikube

subcommands.name: load
subcommands.description: Load a image into minikube

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List images

subcommands.name: pull
subcommands.description: Pull images

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Push images

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more images

subcommands.name: save
subcommands.description: Save a image from minikube

subcommands.name: tag
subcommands.description: Tag images

subcommands.name: addons
subcommands.description: Enable or disable a minikube addon

subcommands.name: configure
subcommands.description: Configures the addon w/ADDON_NAME within minikube

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Disables the addon w/ADDON_NAME within minikube

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Enables the addon w/ADDON_NAME within minikube

subcommands.name: images
subcommands.description: List image names the addon w/ADDON_NAME used

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all available minikube addons as well as their current statuses

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Opens the addon w/ADDON_NAME within minikube

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Modify persistent configuration values

subcommands.name: defaults
subcommands.description: Lists all valid default values for PROPERTY_NAME

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Gets the value of PROPERTY_NAME from the minikube config file

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets an individual value in a minikube config file

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Unsets an individual value in a minikube config file

subcommands.name: view
subcommands.description: Display values currently set in the minikube config file

subcommands.name: profile
subcommands.description: Get or list the current profiles (clusters)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all minikube profiles

subcommands.name: update-context
subcommands.description: Update kubeconfig in case of an IP or port change

subcommands.name: service
subcommands.description: Returns a URL to connect to a service

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists the URLs for the services in your local cluster

options.description: The services namespace
Open the service URL with https instead of http
The initial time interval for each check that wait performs in seconds
The service namespace
Display the Kubernetes service URL in the CLI instead of opening it in the default browser
Amount of time to wait for a service in seconds

subcommands.name: tunnel
subcommands.description: Connect to LoadBalancer services

options.description: Call with cleanup=true to remove old tunnels

subcommands.name: mount
subcommands.description: Mounts the specified directory into minikube

options.description: Specify the 9p version that the mount should use
Default group id used for the mount
Specify the ip that the mount should be setup on
Kill the mount process spawned by minikube start
File permissions used for the mount
The number of bytes to use for 9p packet payload
Additional mount options, such as cache=fscache
Specify the port that the mount should be setup on
Specify the mount filesystem type (supported types: 9p)
Default user id used for the mount

options.description: Use native Golang SSH client
The node to ssh into

subcommands.name: kubectl
subcommands.description: Run a kubectl binary matching the cluster version

options.description: Use SSH for running kubernetes client on the node

subcommands.name: node
subcommands.description: Add, remove, or list additional nodes

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds a node to the given cluster

subcommands.description: Deletes a node from a cluster

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List nodes

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Starts a node

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stops a node in a cluster

options.description: The node to get ssh-key path

The node to ssh into

options.description: The node to get IP

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Returns logs to debug a local Kubernetes cluster

options.description: If present, writes to the provided file instead of stdout
Show only the most recent journal entries
Number of lines back to go within the log
The node to get logs from. Defaults to the primary control plane
Show only log entries which point to known problems
